response.justification: Sarah brings a long, versatile engineering career with genuine high-throughput experience (micro-services handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic), current Staff-level work on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, and strong TypeScript depth — a good match for the AI-native environment. However, the role's must-have of strong Python is not evidenced anywhere on the resume (her systems work is in DLang/C++/Node), the distributed-systems depth is somewhat dated and niche in tooling, and there is little concrete evidence of directing technical strategy, stakeholder coordination, or operational/on-call ownership at Staff scope.
strengths: Hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP/agentic-workflow tooling experience in current Staff role
Real high-throughput, low-latency systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, caching)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing tools authorship, teaching/workshops
concerns: No Python experience listed — a must-have for the role
High-throughput/distributed work was in DLang and ended in 2020; recent roles are product-focused full-stack
Limited evidence of technical-strategy leadership, cross-functional stakeholder management, or on-call operational ownership
